info.name: Recluse, Nature of Arms
info.description: A Subclass for the Reclused based on weapon combat

description.p: You always prefered Armor and Blades

A Recluse sometimes feels drawn towards physical weaponry instead of their magical prowess. They combine what they know with what they can physically learn with what they already know about magic, their body not so frail anymore and now able to wield a sword and wear some armor while managing to imbue elements into their weapons instead of themselves.
By learning to fight with both Magic and Blades you gain the following traits:
Armor Proficiency. You gain proficiency in Light Armor.
Weapon Proficiency. You gain proficiency in Martial Weapons.

Elemental Imbuement. When you absorb an element for your Magic Cocktail, you can choose to put the element into your weapon instead. An imbued weapon deals 2d6 extra damage of the absorbed type the next time you attack with it. The weapon then looses the imbuement.

sheet.description: When you absorb an element, you can choose to imbue a weapon instead. A imbued weapon deals 2d6 extra damage of the absorbed type for the next attack that hits.

description.p: You gain resistances of your choice from Fire, Force, Lightning or Radiant.

Additionally, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack that deals Fire, Force, Lightning or Radiant damage, you can use a reaction to immediately absorb it into your weapon. This trait does not count for damage caused by imbuement. You can use this trait once per round.

sheet.description: If you hit a creature with a weapon attack that deals Fire, Force, Lightning or Radiant damage, you can immediately imbue your weapon with that element. (Does not count for damage caused by imbuement)

description.p: You are resistant to fire damage.
You are resistant to force damage.
You are resistant to lightning damage.
You are resistant to radiant damage.

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of three kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

sheet.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of the weapons you previously chosen for a different one.

description.p: You mastered the way of putting Magic into your weapon, at least to your extend.

Once per turn, after you hit a creature with a weapon attack, choose one of your Recluse Cantrips. The hit target recieves the chosen Cantrips damage and/or effects. This counts as part of the weapon attack (You may still use your Reactive Absorbtion).

sheet.description: If you hit a Creature with a weapon attack, you may have the target be inflicted by the damage/effects of one of your Recluse Cantrips.
